summary: Frontend-only regression for SalonHub luxury reskin (Phase 1: customer-facing). Tested landing page CTAs, customer login flow, salons listing (list/grid/map toggle), salon main page (welcome banner, 3 action cards, 4 stat cards, header buttons), sidebar (open/menu items/theme toggle/logout), theme persistence in localStorage 'salon_theme' with html.dark class, and notification bell navigation. 36/37 assertions passed. No functional regression detected from the visual reskin.
critical_code_review_comments: CustomerLayout.js (~400 lines) is borderline acceptable but combines sidebar + notification fetch + websocket subscription + header chrome. Consider splitting notification logic into a useCustomerNotifications hook to keep layout under 250 lines.

ThemeContext initial state is hardcoded to 'light' before useEffect reads localStorage — causes a brief flash of light theme on first paint for users who saved 'dark'. Initialize via lazy initializer: useState(() => localStorage.getItem('salon_theme') || 'light') and apply .dark class synchronously.

CustomerLayout: hover class 'hover:text-bronze-light' on logout button — verify 'bronze-light' token exists in tailwind.config.js (luxury palette uses bronze + sage). If undefined, the hover state silently no-ops.
SalonSelectionPage logs Geolocation errors to console; consider handling permission denied silently for cleaner console.
WebSocket connection error logged ('socket.io 404') is unrelated to this reskin — pre-existing infra concern.

success_rate.frontend: 97% (36/37 assertions; the 1 PARTIAL is a Playwright timing artifact, not a bug)

test_credentials: Used guest customer login: name='Aanya', phone='9999988888' — works as expected (POST /api/users) and redirects to /salons.
seed_data_creation: None. Used pre-seeded salon id 38d68056-6650-4818-842b-7f6c6fbca46f (The Looks Unisex Salon).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 luxury reskin verified. Customer routes verified: '/', '/login' (note: /user/login is now a redirect to /login), '/salons', '/salon/:id', '/notifications'. All required data-testids are intact: hero-get-started-btn, hero-salon-login-btn, landing-login-btn, footer-customer-login-btn, footer-salon-login-btn, login-name-input, login-phone-input, login-submit-btn, view-list-btn, view-grid-btn, view-map-btn, salon-search-input, city-dropdown-btn, salon-list-item-{id}, salon-card-{id}, sidebar-open-btn, sidebar-close-btn, sidebar-find-a-salon-btn, sidebar-my-history-btn, sidebar-my-profile-btn, sidebar-help-btn, sidebar-report-bug-btn, sidebar-logout-btn, theme-toggle-btn, notifications-bell-btn, dashboard-wallet-card, dashboard-history-card, dashboard-rating-card, dashboard-stylists-card, quick-action-book-btn, quick-action-queue-btn, quick-action-services-btn, salon-back-btn, salon-header-book-btn. Theme toggle confirmed: html.dark class added on dark mode and 'salon_theme' persisted in localStorage. Phase 1 customer pages render in the new charcoal+brass / cream+bronze luxury palette as intended. Admin/dashboard (P3) was not in scope.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The single 'PARTIAL' result on sidebar-close-btn was due to AnimatePresence exit animation not completing within the 500ms wait window of the Playwright assertion — the customer-sidebar element was still present in DOM during animate-out. This is a test timing artifact, not a UI defect; the sidebar visually closes correctly.
